rationales.hedonism: He says "my eyes hurt and I skipped lunch entirely," showing present comfort and enjoyment were pushed aside by work demands.
rationales.achievement: He spent hours fixing the warehouse problem and writing "a detailed process document" to keep operations from failing, which shows strong task focus and competence under pressure.

rationales.hedonism: He takes "late-night shifts that nobody wants" and says it "takes a lot of energy to keep the smile on," sacrificing comfort and ease for other priorities.
rationales.achievement: He is "mapping out which restaurants have the fastest pickup times" and tracking a "4.92" rating, showing active optimization and performance monitoring.
rationales.security: He focuses on "more consistent income," counts that savings will last "three months, maybe four," and adjusts work to protect the household against possible lost hours.
rationales.conformity: He works to stay above the app's rating threshold and describes having to "keep the smile on," showing deliberate effort to meet platform and customer expectations.
rationales.benevolence: His extra late-night shifts are framed around his wife's possible January hour cuts and the need to protect shared finances, showing concrete support for a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: He made "an actual meal," opened wine, and notes "It was genuinely good," showing clear investment in sensory pleasure and present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He immediately turns the meal into "mental arithmetic" about how the time "could have been two hours in the library" for a chapter "already a week behind," showing pleasure being judged against productivity demands.

rationales.stimulation: She stops herself from exploring travel nursing because she is afraid it "might actually be doable," backing away from a new, riskier life she finds exciting.
rationales.security: She admits the real barrier is not wanting "to give up what I already know," which clearly favors familiarity, predictability, and keeping life stable.
